sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 18.47 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.18)

statement.latex: Is it algorithmically decidable whether a group generated by three given class transpositions (for the definition, see 17.57) [r]{a)} has only finite orbits on \( \)? [r]{b)} acts transitively on the set of nonnegative integers in its support? A difficult case is the group \( _{1(2),4(6)}, _{1(3),2(6)}, _{2(3),4(6)} \), which acts transitively on \( 0(6)\) if and only if Collatz' \(3n+1\) conjectu...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
